title: BURN ANGER BEFORE ANGER BURNS YOU | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live

segments.text: Hare Krishna! A warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session. The topic
for the day is, Burn Anger Before Anger Burns You. If you remember, couple of weeks back
we had a topic, Ame Gussa Kio Aata Hai? And while we were discussing about anger, a lot
of questions came forth about how to go about controlling our temper, more so in present
day to day circumstances. We all get angry at times, sometimes situation can be very
provoking. So today's session is all about how we can control our anger in such trying
circumstances. I will share with you some tips, some techniques, some things which might
And again, it's from the teachings of Bhagavad Gita. Krishna has given us many, many practical
insights, how we can control our base tendencies, lust, greed, pride, anger, envy, jealousy.
I will quote few of the verses from the Gita while I share these tips with you. So we will
begin the session today with prayers to Lord Krishna.

you might win. You might win the argument but we lose a relationship. So, when we are getting
angry in that moment, check if we can have a good sense to press the pause button,
extricate from that situation and come back and handle the situation with a cool head.
It is said that do not reply to any emails in an angry mood. Suppose somebody has sent an email,
it's offended you, do not reply immediately. Let some time pass, pull off. So, that's
a practical suggestion. And the last thing, it is very, very important, very, very important,
if somebody has offended us, we do not brew hatred, anger, ill feelings against that person
in our heart. Momentarily, yes, we can get angry and yes, we can even later on if we were wrong,
we can go and ask forgiveness. But last what we should do is brew hatred, anger, ill feeling
against some person for what that person has done to us. In other words, a heart should be clean.
Once there was a teacher who gave an assignment to all the students in the class
that tomorrow get a plastic bag from your home and in the plastic bag get some potatoes,
on each potato write a person's name against whom you have some grudge, some hatred or some anger.
And if you are grudges quite a lot, you're angry at a person quite, you have a bigger potato.
And then if it is just a minor grudge, you can have a smaller potato. But have one potato for
one person and if you have grudge with five people, you have anger against five people, have five
people and bring these two potatoes. And bring to your classroom in a plastic bag.
So next day the students came back to the classroom. Somebody in a plastic
bag having eight potatoes, somebody having three potatoes, somebody having five potatoes
and everyone having the name written of the person on the potato with a sketch pen.
So the teacher gave them an assignment. He says for next
carry this bag of potatoes with you even when you have to go to a restroom, always carry
this bag with you, even when you go to the bed make sure that this bag is next to you.
So knowing that the teacher has given this assignment and there is some important learning
which the teacher is trying to give, all the students very dutifully obeyed.
So all of them were seen carrying this bag with potatoes, some of them 6 potatoes, some
of them 8 potatoes, some of them 10 of them and 2 days, 3 days, 4 days the potatoes in
that plastic bag started rotting, that stench started emanating from that bag and at times
it became very very, it became difficult to tolerate that stench but somehow they were
counting when this assignment will get over, another 2 days left, another 1 day left, another
2 days left and finally when the time was announced the assignment is over, they felt
Now they can get rid of this bag which is full of potatoes with lot of bad smell, rotten
potatoes smelling so badly and they have to carry all along with them.
So then came the time when teacher gave them the lesson.
Just like this bag of potatoes and these potatoes are rotting and it is creating some foul smell
and we are not able to tolerate.
Imagine in our mind body system if we carry hatred, anger, ill feelings against some person
who has done some wrong to us, that foul smell, that hatred will burn us from within.

It is killing us from within, it is burning us from within and we may not be even aware

And for sure, we will try to answer almost all the questions which have come forth in today's
session and also the previous session. The first question is from Pawan. After reading Bhagavad
Gita, I found that I always get angry and lusty thoughts in the afternoon time. Can you please
help me how to handle afternoon time? Generally, morning time is said to be predominantly
sattva guna. Afternoon time, evening time is, raja guna is predominant and night time, tamo guna is
predominant, generally speaking. That's why you have bars, pubs, all these discotheques all operating
in the night time. Temples open in the morning time. So generally, in the afternoon, as we know
that raja guna will be predominant. Just a small tip which I can give.
You can try taking a shower in the afternoon, taking a bath because it also invokes sattva
guna. If you want, you can try taking a small power nap, small nap because when we get tired,
our brain gets tired, our mind gets tired, we start feeling jittery, all this raja guna.
So try if you can take a bath, take some deep breaths and read some Gita. All that will definitely
help to not fall prey to the lower modes.
This question is from Manish Kumar. What is wrong in sense gratification which is not
sinful like visiting nice places, availing good medical treatment? Yes, sense gratification
which is not sinful, which is very much within the domain of, Krishna is also not unreasonable.
we are meeting someone and we are working within some boundaries, it is perfectly justified.
So we do not have to feel guilty if you have gone to a nice garden for having a stroll
and you are enjoying the beauty of a garden, nothing wrong. But try if you can think that
this beautiful garden is a creation of the Lord. If you are eating some nice thing, be
grateful to the Lord and see that this wonderful foodstuff is being supplied by the Lord. So
we can be Krishna conscious. We can be Krishna conscious. We can be Krishna conscious. We
can be Krishna conscious while even we try to enjoy some of the so-called pleasures of
This question is from Vikas Sharma. We encounter situations where the person is using offensive
language. Should we just remain silent and control the anger? It is not recommended that
in every situation we have to tolerate. If you remember in our previous session we talked
Specifically, if the devotees and the Lord is being offended or somebody is using an abusive
language, it is very much justified to become angry. But assuming somebody is saying some words
to us and I am getting hurt, I am getting hurt, my ego is getting hurt, I should tolerate. Again,
it depends on the situation, if a person is taking undue advantage as a matter of duty,
we can speak to that person, we can even retaliate as a matter of duty. We can use
anger as a tool rather than we being controlled by anger. There is a difference between the two,
anger being used as a tool vis a vis anger using us, we being slaves of our anger is
We can speak to him, we can even use harsh words, please mind your language, please do
not act like that, it is not at all acceptable, we can be very stern in our behaviour.
So Krishna Consciousness does not mean that you just tolerate, tolerate and tolerate,
we have to use our intelligence and act.

She has to behave in the way I want her to behave.

This question is from Ashtesh Kumar. Prabhuji, what if we are with a person daily who is
not realizing that he is doing something which is making us angry? We cannot control persons
beyond a point. We cannot control situations beyond a point. We cannot control circumstances
beyond a point. But what we can control is how we respond to those situations.
that is within our circle of influence. So, rather than saying he is not behaving like that,
he is acting in that way, how can I change that person? What can I do to take care of his
behavior? The question should be asked how I can respond intelligently. No doubt as a matter of
duty, we can try to correct a person. We can counsel that person if he is junior, we can
tell him very politely what is wrong in his behavior as a matter of duty. But the crux is
what we can do ourselves to respond to that situation intelligently in a Krishna conscious.
This question is from Bhavik Jain. Why negative thoughts comes to the mind even if we are not
thinking about it? Because we have a conscious mind and we have a subconscious mind.
Subconscious mind is a storehouse of all our experiences, all our activities,
vasanas of the past. So, many times they all come up. For example, you may not be dabbling
with lusty thoughts in the present but what about all what we have seen and dabbled with in the
past? All that has stored. So, sometimes in some circumstances the lusty thoughts can get triggered,
and all those memories can start popping up in our mind-body system. So, that is why we have to
learn to tolerate while we are practicing the process which will purify our conscious mind
and subconscious mind. This question is from Nikita Nandani. Please suggest the chapter in
Bhagavad Gita for relationship management taboo.
Frankly speaking, I will have to check which chapter has verses which specifically talk about
relationship. But in general, I would like to mention Bhagavad Gita may not give a very
pinpointed specific answers to specific problems. Like for example, anger management, verse number,
so and so, so and so and so. It is giving us a perspective of life, an empowered perspective of
life. It is giving us a perspective of life, an empowered perspective of life. It is giving us a
perspective of life, a paradigm, the knowledge by which if we act we will be able to solve all the
problems of life, all the problems which we encounter in life.
Mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishasi. Krishna says, if you become conscious of me,
by my grace, by my blessings, you will cross over all obstacles. He is not saying relationship
problem, health problem, anger problem. He will cross over all obstacles. He will cross over all
obstacles of conditional life by my grace. So this process of Krishna consciousness is how we can
take refuge of the Lord, how we can lead a life following the instructions of the Lord by His
blessings, by His mercy, by His guidance. Krishna says, from me comes knowledge,
remembrance and forgetfulness. By right guidance which comes from the Lord,
mystically, dynamically, we can cross all the problems which we might encounter in our lives.
That's why we say Bhagavad Gita is a solution to all the problems, not just relationship problem,
not just health problem, not some interpersonal problems. All the problems, Bhagavad Gita has
solutions if we follow the principles which Krishna is sharing with us in the Gita.
